name: Bryce Lance
headline: Fourth-round Saints rookie is a dynasty stash, not a 2026 starter
body:
The Saints used a fourth-round pick on a 6-foot-3, 204-pound receiver who posted back-to-back 1,000-yard seasons at North Dakota State and tested like an athlete, running 4.34 with a 41-inch vertical. The college production and frame are real, but so is the traffic ahead of him: Chris Olave and Jordyn Tyson headline the room, with Juwan Johnson commanding targets from the slot and tight end spot. That logjam buries Lance on the depth chart, and the projection of roughly 16 catches for 204 yards and a score reflects a player fighting for snaps rather than touches.

For redraft purposes he's essentially undraftable, sitting around pick 298 ADP and rostered in about 1% of leagues. The dynasty case is sharper. A 61.5% contested-catch rate and a 3.8% drop rate point to reliable hands, and if Olave moves on after 2026, Lance has a clear runway to a WR2 role. Treat him as a late rookie-draft flier, not a contributor.
